Data Preprocessing

Raw data

Table 1. Code book.
col_name description col_type
id Patient identifier c
pe Preeclampsia (0=no; 1=yes) f
iugr Intrauterine growth restriction (0=no; 1=yes) f
age Maternal age (years) d
weight Pre-pregnancy weight (kg) d
height Maternal height (m) d
bmi BMI (kg/m²) d
uta_ri_r Art ut. D-resistance index d
uta_pi_r Art ut. D-pulsatility index d
uta_psv_r Art ut. D-peak systolic velocity d
uta_ri_l Art ut. L-resistance index d
uta_pi_l Art ut. L-pulsatility index d
uta_psv_l Art ut. L-peak systolic velocity d
uta_ri Art ut. mean-resistance index d
uta_pi Art ut. mean-pulsatility index d
uta_psv Art ut. mean-peak systolic velocity d
uta_bn Art ut. bilateral notch d
ga Gestational age at delivery (weeks) d
parity Parity d
bw Birth weight (g) d
sflt1 S-Flt1 (µg/L) d
plgf S-PLGF (µg/L) d
sflt1_plgf sFLT/PLGF d 
## Rows:  95
## Columns:  23

Select variable of interest

Data partition

## Figure 1. Data partition.

Data vizualization

One-variable visualization

QQ plot

## Number of QQ plots (= # of numeric variables):  20
qq_plotter <- function(variable, data){
  variable_lab <-
    case_when(
       variable %in% skew_r_var ~ paste0("sqrt(", variable, ")")
       , variable %in% skew_l_var ~ paste0(variable, "^2")
       , variable %in% tail_heavy_var ~ paste0("log(", variable, ")")
       , TRUE ~ variable
    )
  
  data |>
    `colnames<-`(str_replace_all(colnames(data), variable, "variable")) |>
    ggplot(aes(sample = variable)) +
    geom_qq() +
    geom_qq_line() +
    xlab("Normal +/- SD") +
    ylab(variable_lab) +
    theme(axis.title = element_text(size = 10))
}

## Figure 2. QQ plots.

Bar chart

## Number of bar charts (= # of categorical variables):  2
bar_chart_plotter <- function(variable, data){
  data |>
    `colnames<-`(str_replace_all(colnames(data), variable, "variable")) |>
    group_by(variable) |>
    summarize(n = n()) |>
    mutate(p = n / sum(n) * 100) |>
    ggplot(aes(variable, p)) +
    geom_col() +
    xlab(variable) +
    ylab("Proportion (%)") +
    theme(axis.title = element_text(size = 10))
}

## Figure 3. Bar charts.

Two-to-three-variable visualization

Scatter plot

## Only these numeric variables are visualized:
## [1] "age"     "bmi"     "uta_ri"  "uta_pi"  "uta_psv" "sflt1"   "plgf"
## Number of scatter plots (= # of possible pairs of num. var.):  21
scatter_plotter <- function(pair, data, third = NULL){
  if(is.null(third)){
    sc_plot <-
      data |>
      select_at(pair) |>
      `colnames<-`(c("V1", "V2")) |>
      ggplot(aes(V1, V2))
  }else{
    sc_plot <-
      data |>
      select_at(c(pair, third)) |>
      `colnames<-`(c("V1", "V2", "V3")) |>
      ggplot(aes(V1, V2, color = V3))
  }
  
  sc_plot <-
    sc_plot +
    geom_point(show.legend = FALSE)  +
    xlab(pair[1]) +
    ylab(pair[2]) +
    theme(axis.title = element_text(size = 10))
  
  if(!is.null(third)){
    sc_plot <-
      sc_plot +
      scale_color_discrete(third)
  }
  
  return(sc_plot)
}

## Figure 4. Scatter plots.

## Figure 4. Scatter plots.

Box and violin plots

## Only these categorical variables are visualized:
## [1] "outcome" "iugr"
## Number of box plots (= # of possible pairs of num-cat. var.):  14
box_plotter <- function(pair, data){
  bx_plot <-
    data |>
    select_at(pair) |>
    `colnames<-`(c("V1", "V2")) |>
    ggplot(aes(V1, V2)) +
    geom_boxplot(outliers = FALSE)
  
  set.seed(seed)
  bx_plot <-
    bx_plot +
    geom_jitter(width = 0.2)
  
  bx_plot <-
    bx_plot +
    xlab(pair[1]) +
    ylab(pair[2]) +
    theme(axis.title = element_text(size = 10))
  
  return(bx_plot)
}

## Figure 5. Box plots.
violin_plotter <- function(pair, data){
  vl_plot <-
    data |>
    select_at(pair) |>
    `colnames<-`(c("V1", "V2")) |>
    ggplot(aes(V1, V2)) +
    geom_violin()
  
  set.seed(seed)
  vl_plot <-
    vl_plot +
    geom_jitter(width = 0.2)
  
  vl_plot <-
    vl_plot +
    xlab(pair[1]) +
    ylab(pair[2]) +
    theme(axis.title = element_text(size = 10))
  
  return(vl_plot)
}

## Figure 6. Violin plots.

Stacked bar chart

## Number of stacked bar charts (= # of poss. pairs of cat. var.):  1
sb_chart_plotter <- function(pair, data, mode = "proportion"){
  
  data <-
    data |>
    select_at(pair) |>
    `colnames<-`(c("V1", "V2")) |>
    group_by(V1, V2) |>
    summarize(n = n(), .groups = "drop")
  
  if(mode == "proportion"){
    
  }
  
  if(mode == "count"){
    data <-
      data |>
      mutate(y = n)
    
    ylab_txt <- "Count"
  }else{
    data <-
      data |>
      group_by(V1) |>
      mutate(y = n / sum(n) * 100)
    
    ylab_txt <- "Proportion (%)"
  }
  
  data |>
    ggplot(aes(x = V1, y = y, fill = V2)) +
    geom_col() +
    xlab(pair[1]) +
    ylab(ylab_txt) +
    scale_fill_discrete(name = pair[2]) +
    theme(
      axis.title = element_text(size = 10)
      , legend.title = element_text(size = 10)
    )
}

## Figure 7. Stacked bar charts.

## Figure 8. Stacked bar charts by count.

More-than-three-variable visualization

Sample clustering

pc_hclust_pca_plotter <- function(pc_data, h_cluster, pca_fit){
  
  var_exp <-
    summary(pca_fit)$importance["Proportion of Variance", 1:2] * 100
  
  pc_data |>
    mutate(cluster = factor(h_cluster)) |>
    ggplot(aes(x = PC1, y = PC2, color = cluster)) +
    geom_point(size = 3) +
    labs(
      x = paste0("PC1 (", round(var_exp[1], 1), "%)")
      , y = paste0("PC2 (", round(var_exp[2], 1), "%)")
      , color = "Hierarchical cluster"
    )
}

## Figure 9. PC-HC plot.
pc_hclust_plotter <- function(hclust_fit, h_cluster){
  h_cluster <- factor(h_cluster)
  cols <- as.integer(h_cluster)

  plot(hclust_fit, cex = 0.6)

  labels <- hclust_fit$labels
  order <- hclust_fit$order
  usr <- par("usr")

  y1 <- usr[3] - 0.02 * diff(usr[3:4])
  y2 <- usr[3] - 0.06 * diff(usr[3:4])

  text(
    x = seq_along(order)
    , y = y2
    , labels = h_cluster[order]
    , srt = 90
    , adj = 1
    , xpd = NA
    , cex = 0.6
    , col = cols[order]
  )
}

## Figure 10. PC-hclust plot.
